priorities.message: The arms bend unevenly, so one side takes more of the load through the rep.
priorities.tip: Lower evenly and keep the chest square to the floor.

priorities.message: The two arms move through different ranges — a sign of a strength or stability imbalance.
priorities.tip: Match left and right depth; add a short pause at the bottom to even them out.

priorities.message: The hips sag (or pike), breaking the straight plank line and shifting load onto the lower back instead of the chest and core.
priorities.tip: Squeeze the glutes and brace the core to hold one rigid line from shoulders to ankles.

fault_groups.message: The arms bend unevenly, so one side takes more of the load through the rep.
fault_groups.tip: Lower evenly and keep the chest square to the floor.

fault_groups.message: The two arms move through different ranges — a sign of a strength or stability imbalance.
fault_groups.tip: Match left and right depth; add a short pause at the bottom to even them out.

fault_groups.message: The hips sag (or pike), breaking the straight plank line and shifting load onto the lower back instead of the chest and core.
fault_groups.tip: Squeeze the glutes and brace the core to hold one rigid line from shoulders to ankles.

priorities.message: The wrist flexes or extends excessively, bleeding force into the forearm and stressing the wrist joint.
priorities.tip: Keep the wrist neutral and stacked over the forearm throughout.

priorities.message: The elbow drifts forward during the curl (shown side), recruiting the front delt and shortening the biceps' line of pull.
priorities.tip: Pin the elbow against your side and just in front of the ribs; only the forearm should move.

priorities.message: The upper arm swings instead of staying fixed, using the shoulder to help lift the weight.
priorities.tip: Keep the upper arm vertical and motionless; if it has to move, the weight is too heavy.

fault_groups.message: The wrist flexes or extends excessively, bleeding force into the forearm and stressing the wrist joint.
fault_groups.tip: Keep the wrist neutral and stacked over the forearm throughout.

fault_groups.message: The elbow drifts forward during the curl (shown side), recruiting the front delt and shortening the biceps' line of pull.
fault_groups.tip: Pin the elbow against your side and just in front of the ribs; only the forearm should move.

fault_groups.message: The upper arm swings instead of staying fixed, using the shoulder to help lift the weight.
fault_groups.tip: Keep the upper arm vertical and motionless; if it has to move, the weight is too heavy.

fault_groups.message: One arm reaches the top noticeably later than the other, so the set is really two out-of-sync arms.
fault_groups.tip: Curl both arms together to a shared count; slow down so the lagging arm keeps pace.

priorities.message: The torso sways to swing the weight up, using the whole body instead of isolating the delts.
priorities.tip: Stand tall and brace; keep the trunk still so only the arms move.

priorities.message: One arm is consistently higher than the other, so the delts are trained unevenly.
priorities.tip: Raise both arms to the same height; film head-on and match them.

priorities.message: One arm reaches the top later than the other — the lift is out of sync side to side.
priorities.tip: Raise both arms together to a shared count.

fault_groups.message: The torso sways to swing the weight up, using the whole body instead of isolating the delts.
fault_groups.tip: Stand tall and brace; keep the trunk still so only the arms move.

fault_groups.message: One arm is consistently higher than the other, so the delts are trained unevenly.
fault_groups.tip: Raise both arms to the same height; film head-on and match them.

fault_groups.message: One arm reaches the top later than the other — the lift is out of sync side to side.
fault_groups.tip: Raise both arms together to a shared count.

priorities.message: The shoulders round forward of the hips at setup/early pull (approximate, shown side), softening the upper back.
priorities.tip: Pull the shoulders back and down and keep the upper back tight before you pull.

priorities.message: The bar (from wrist path, shown side) swings away from the body, lengthening the lever arm and stressing the lower back.
priorities.tip: Keep the bar dragging up the legs; cue the lats to pull it back into you.

priorities.message: One side extends ahead of the other, so the bar tips and one leg/hip is doing more of the work.
priorities.tip: Grip evenly, brace both sides, and drive through both legs equally.

fault_groups.message: The shoulders round forward of the hips at setup/early pull (approximate, shown side), softening the upper back.
fault_groups.tip: Pull the shoulders back and down and keep the upper back tight before you pull.

fault_groups.message: The bar (from wrist path, shown side) swings away from the body, lengthening the lever arm and stressing the lower back.
fault_groups.tip: Keep the bar dragging up the legs; cue the lats to pull it back into you.

fault_groups.message: One side extends ahead of the other, so the bar tips and one leg/hip is doing more of the work.
fault_groups.tip: Grip evenly, brace both sides, and drive through both legs equally.
